title: ADR: build custom coordinator vs. run bigger-pi as a BOINC project
context: PLAN.md v0.2 adds open question #8: whether to build a custom coordinator from scratch or to run bigger-pi as a BOINC project. BOINC already solves work distribution, redundant validation, and credit. The plan explicitly frames this as a milestone-sized decision to be resolved at or before M2, noting that the Hee-Lee Oss computeBudgetCpuHours schema gap (feat-023) must also be resolved before M2. The choice has second-order effects on the coordinator (coord-011), worker client (worker-012), redundancy factor design, and the abuse guardrails (abuse-013). The ADR must land before M2 implementation begins.
objective: Produce an ADR that evaluates building a custom coordinator vs. adopting BOINC as the distribution/validation/credit layer for bigger-pi, and records the decision with rationale.
acceptanceCriteria: Evaluates the build-vs-reuse trade-off: custom coordinator (full control, no external dependency, more build effort) vs. BOINC (work distribution, redundant validation, credit already solved; external dependency and BOINC-project onboarding overhead).
Documents which BOINC capabilities would be reused or replaced in each path, and how the determinism contract, HARD RULE verification gate, and abuse guardrails are satisfied in each path.
Documents the impact on the coordinator (coord-011), worker client (worker-012), and the redundancy/quorum design.
Records a single decision (custom or BOINC) with rationale, trade-offs, and any conditions or constraints; reviewed by the maintainer before M2 begins.
output: An ADR document for the build-vs-reuse (custom coordinator vs. BOINC) decision that gates M2.
